ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for dacquoise or Oreo dacquoise; 'cake food product' (FOODON_03301116) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries and similar baked products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cakes and pastries as a category; no specific code for dacquoise exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to dacquoise or Oreo dacquoise cake; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Oreo Dacquoise as served at a Singapore patisserie, approximately 80 g, consisting of two to three almond meringue layers sandwiched with Oreo-flavoured whipped cream.

nutrient_source.title: Dacquoise cake — estimated from component ingredients: almond meringue (egg white, almond flour, sugar) and Oreo cream filling, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for meringue cookies (FDC ID 169938), almond flour (FDC ID 169426), whipping cream (FDC ID 170859), and Oreo cookies (FDC ID 358890)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Oreo Dacquoise exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of component ingredients: approximately 35% almond meringue shell (egg white, almond flour, icing sugar), 40% Oreo-flavoured whipped cream filling (heavy cream, crushed Oreo), and 25% Oreo cookie crumb layer. Proportions are based on standard dacquoise construction. Values are cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy entries for each component. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cake and pastry products — analogous estimate based on 'sponge cake' (GI 46) and 'chocolate cake with frosting' (GI 38–54) from the University of Sydney GI Database; adjusted upward to 55 for high refined-sugar and Oreo cookie content
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Oreo Dacquoise. The GI is estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous cake products in the University of Sydney GI Database: sponge cake GI ~46, chocolate cake with frosting GI ~38–54. While 55 is at the upper end of the low GI category, the high refined-sugar and Oreo cookie content contribute to this value. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 34.4) / 100 ≈ 19.0, where carbohydrate per 80 g serving = 43.0 × 0.80 = 34.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and high sugar load
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 115 mg sodium per 100 g (92 mg per 80 g serving), a single piece of Oreo Dacquoise contributes a modest sodium load, but the high sugar content (32 g/100 g) may promote weight gain and insulin resistance, which are secondary drivers of elevated blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one piece and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined sugar and glycemic load despite low GI
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic index of 55 (categorized as low) and a glycemic load of approximately 19 per 80 g serving, Oreo Dacquoise still poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar content; consumption should be occasional and limited to half a piece, paired with a protein or fibre source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Oreo Dacquoise contains approximately 10.5 g saturated fat and 0.2 g trans fat per 100 g, alongside 32 g of sugar, all of which are established risk factors for dyslipidaemia and atherosclerosis; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit intake to no more than one small piece per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oreo Dacquoise is a contemporary patisserie dessert sold in Singapore specialty cake shops and premium cafes. It consists of two or three crisp almond meringue discs (dacquoise layers) sandwiched with a rich Oreo-flavoured whipped cream or buttercream filling, and is typically finished with crushed Oreo cookie crumbs on the exterior. The dacquoise shell is made by folding almond flour and icing sugar into stiff egg-white meringue, then baking at low temperature until the exterior is crisp and the interior remains slightly chewy. The Oreo cream filling is prepared by whipping heavy cream with crushed Oreo cookies and sometimes cream cheese or butter to achieve a stable, pipeable texture. The result is a dessert that contrasts the light, nutty crunch of the meringue with the sweet, chocolatey richness of the Oreo cream. Nutritionally, Oreo Dacquoise is energy-dense at approximately 390 kcal per 100 g, with significant contributions from fat (22 g, of which 10.5 g is saturated) and refined sugar (32 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard 80 g serving. This dessert is not suitable for frequent consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to its high saturated fat and sugar content, despite its low GI classification being at the upper threshold. It contains common allergens including eggs, almonds, milk, wheat, and soy. It is vegetarian but not vegan or gluten-free. In Singapore, this dessert is associated with premium patisserie culture and is commonly purchased as a single-serve slice or as a whole cake for gifting.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Oreo Dacquoise exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of component ingredients (almond meringue, whipped cream, Oreo cookies) cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using comparable cake products. Confidence is medium because the estimation methodology is systematic and cross-referenced, but no single approved source directly measures this specific product.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for estimation. Vitamin C is 0.0 as this is a baked meringue and cream dessert containing no fruit or vitamin-C-rich ingredients; confirmed zero by ingredient composition. Trans fat of 0.2 g per 100 g is estimated from the Oreo cookie component (partially hydrogenated oils in commercial cookie formulations); this value may vary by specific product batch. Halal status is listed as false because Oreo cookies sold in Singapore may or may not carry MUIS halal certification depending on the specific product variant used by the restaurant; consumers should verify with the vendor. The serving size of 80 g is an estimate for one standard patisserie slice; actual weight may vary between 70 g and 100 g depending on the vendor. Glycemic load per serving = (55 × 34.4) / 100 ≈ 19.0, where 34.4 g is the carbohydrate content of an 80 g serving (43.0 × 0.80). All -1 sentinel values reflect nutrients not reported in the component source data used for estimation.
